title: Vibration test — scope and planning constraints
content: The vibration test assesses cell mechanical integrity and electrical stability under sinusoidal or random vibration profiles representative of transport or field deployment. The chapter is an outline reference only; an executable procedure must come from the applicable standard (UN38.3, IEC 62660-2, or project-specific requirement). The SOC, test axis, amplitude, frequency range, and post-test observation period must all be specified and reviewed before the test is released.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [S1] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Vibration," White Paper, 2018, pp. 63-65.

title: Mechanical shock test — scope and planning constraints
content: Half-sine or trapezoidal shock pulse applied to the cell or module to evaluate structural and electrical integrity. Pulse shape, amplitude (g), duration, and number of shocks per axis are standard-dependent. The chapter is an outline reference. A project SOP specifying the applicable standard must be produced before release.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [S2] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Mechanical shock," White Paper, 2018, pp. 66-67.

title: Drop test — scope and planning constraints
content: Drop test assesses impact tolerance when the cell is released onto a hard surface from a defined height. The chapter is an outline; drop height, surface specification, SOC, orientation, and post-drop observation criteria must come from the applicable standard and be reviewed before release.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [S3] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Drop," White Paper, 2018, p. 68.

title: Cell impact / crush test — scope and planning constraints
content: Simulates internal short circuit via external mechanical deformation. Requires hydraulic press or dedicated crush fixture. A destructive test requiring EUCAR hazard level pre-assessment, fire-safe containment, and gas measurement. The chapter is an outline reference; a reviewed SOP is mandatory before release.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [S4] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Cell impact / crush," White Paper, 2018, p. 69.

title: Nail penetration test — scope
content: The penetration test per SAE J2464:2009 simulates internal short circuit via a conductive steel nail driven through the fully charged cell at ≥ 8 cm/s. The test is applicable to all chemistries, relevant for dendrite and foreign-object fault scenarios. It is performed at cell level (3 mm nail) or module/pack level (20 mm nail). Toxic and flammable gas release must be measured.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [S5] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Penetration," White Paper, 2018, pp. 70-73.

title: Nail penetration test — key execution parameters
content: Charge cell to 100% SOC. Wait for thermal equilibrium at room temperature. Penetrate with a mild steel nail perpendicular to the electrodes at ≥ 8 cm/s. Cell: 3 mm nail, tapered point, full depth. Module: 20 mm nail, through at least 3 cells or 100 mm. Observe for 1 hour with nail in place. Log at ≥ 10 Hz. After 1 h, record voltage, temperatures, and any venting or fire events.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [S5] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Penetration," White Paper, 2018, pp. 70-73.

title: Roll-over test — scope and planning constraints
content: Assesses cell or module safety after an inversion or roll-over event, relevant to vehicle crash scenarios. The chapter is an outline; SOC, inversion angle, and observation period must be specified by the applicable standard and reviewed before release.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [S6] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Roll-over," White Paper, 2018, p. 74.

title: Temperature cycling test — scope and planning constraints
content: Repeated thermal excursions between defined temperature extremes to assess cell or module integrity. The chapter is an outline reference. A reviewed SOP specifying temperature range, ramp rate, soak time, number of cycles, and pass/fail criteria (per IEC 62133, UN38.3, or alternate standard) is required before release.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [T1] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Temperature cycling," White Paper, 2018, pp. 75-77.

title: High temperature endurance — scope and planning constraints
content: Prolonged exposure at elevated temperature to assess safety and capacity retention. Different from calendar ageing in intent: this is an abuse/endurance test targeting a specific standard pass/fail threshold, not a degradation characterisation campaign. Reviewed SOP required.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [T2] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: High temperature endurance," White Paper, 2018, p. 78.

title: External short circuit test — scope
content: Determines cell/module safety under an external short circuit — an abusive out-of-operation scenario simulating vehicle crash or maintenance fault. The test is performed at SOC 100% with a short-circuit resistance ≤ 5 mΩ. Goal is to trigger or bypass internal overcurrent protection and characterise the resulting thermal stress. Applicable to all levels from cell to system.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [T3] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: External short circuit," White Paper, 2018, pp. 82-85.

title: External short circuit test — key execution parameters
content: Bring cell to 100% SOC. Store at room temperature. Apply short circuit by connecting positive and negative terminals with external resistance ≤ 5 mΩ for 10 min (or per standard: UN38.3 = 1 h at 55 °C; IEC 62660-2 = 10 min + 1 h observation). Measure current at ≥ 20 kHz. Log temperature at ≥ 10 Hz. Observe for 1 h after removing the short. Record removed charge and voltage recovery.

standard_variants.IEC62133: similar to UN38.3, different sample count

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [T3] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: External short circuit," White Paper, 2018, pp. 82-85.

title: Overcharge test — scope and planning constraints
content: Charges the cell beyond the maximum rated voltage to assess safety response. A destructive abuse test requiring fire-safe chamber, gas measurement, and full EUCAR hazard pre-assessment. Reviewed SOP with target overcharge voltage, charge rate, and termination condition required before release.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [T4] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Overcharge," White Paper, 2018, pp. 86-88.

title: Forced discharge test — scope and planning constraints
content: Discharges the cell below its rated minimum voltage into reversal. Assesses safety response (gas generation, thermal event). Reversal current, depth, and abort criteria are standard-dependent. Reviewed SOP required.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [T5] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Forced discharge," White Paper, 2018, p. 89.

title: Internal short circuit test — scope and planning constraints
content: Induces a controlled internal short circuit to study initiation and safety response. Trigger methods include nail, separator defect film, or shape-memory alloy pins. The trigger method and severity must be specified in a reviewed SOP. Requires containment, gas measurement, and EUCAR hazard pre-assessment.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [T6] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Internal short circuit," White Paper, 2018, pp. 92-93.

title: Thermal runaway propagation test — scope and planning constraints
content: Module or pack level test assessing whether thermal runaway initiated in one cell propagates to adjacent cells. Requires multiple temperature sensors per cell, gas measurement, certified fire-safe chamber, and a reviewed SOP specifying the trigger method, propagation decision criteria, and gas venting analysis approach.

citation.linked_reference_markdown: [T7] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Propagation of thermal runaway," White Paper, 2018, pp. 90-91.
